content:
# Numbers Chapter 34 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 192-193 | **Source Image:** `NUM_034_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_034_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0140-pa_0141

---

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**2** "Command the sons of Israel and say to them: When you enter into the land of Canaan, this is the land that shall fall to you as an inheritance — the land of Canaan according to its boundaries.

**3** And your southern side shall be from the wilderness of Zin alongside Edom, and your southern boundary shall be from the end of the Salt Sea on the east.

**4** And your boundary shall turn from the south to the ascent of Akrabbim, and pass over to Zin, and its going out shall be from the south to Kadesh-barnea, and it shall go out to Hazar-addar, and pass over to Azmon.

**5** And the boundary shall turn from Azmon to the Brook of Egypt, and its going out shall be toward the sea.

**6** And as for the western boundary, you shall have the Great Sea as a boundary; this shall be your western boundary.

**7** And this shall be your northern boundary: from the Great Sea you shall mark out for yourselves Mount Hor.

**8** From Mount Hor you shall mark out to the entrance of Hamath, and the going out of the boundary shall be toward Zedad.

**9** And the boundary shall go out to Ziphron, and its going out shall be at Hazar-enan. This shall be your northern boundary.

**10** And you shall mark out your eastern boundary from Hazar-enan to Shepham.

**11** And the boundary shall go down from Shepham to Riblah, on the east side of Ain. And the boundary shall go down and strike against the slope of the Sea of Chinnereth on the east.

**12** And the boundary shall go down to the Jordan, and its going out shall be at the Salt Sea. This shall be your land according to its boundaries all around."

**13** And Moses commanded the sons of Israel, saying, "This is the land which you shall inherit by lot, which YHWH has commanded to give to the nine tribes and to the half-tribe.

**14** For the tribe of the sons of Reuben according to the house of their fathers, and the tribe of the sons of Gad according to the house of their fathers, have received their inheritance; and the half-tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance.

**15** The two tribes and the half-tribe have received their inheritance beyond the Jordan, opposite Jericho, eastward, toward the sunrise."

**16** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**17** "These are the names of the men who shall divide the land for you as an inheritance: Eleazar the priest and Joshua the son of Nun.

**18** And you shall take one leader from each tribe to divide the land for an inheritance.

**19** And these are the names of the men: for the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh.

**20** And for the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Shemuel the son of Ammihud.

**21** For the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad the son of Chislon.

**22** And for the tribe of the sons of Dan, a leader: Bukki the son of Jogli.

**23** For the sons of Joseph: for the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, a leader: Hanniel the son of Ephod.

**24** And for the tribe of the sons of Ephraim, a leader: Kemuel the son of Shiphtan.

**25** And for the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, a leader: Elizaphan the son of Parnach.

**26** And for the tribe of the sons of Issachar, a leader: Paltiel the son of Azzan.

**27** And for the tribe of the sons of Asher, a leader: Ahihud the son of Shelomi.

**28** And for the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, a leader: Pedahel the son of Ammihud.

**29** These are the ones whom YHWH commanded to divide the inheritance for the sons of Israel in the land of Canaan."

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **YHWH (vv. 1, 13, 16, 29):** The Tetragrammaton (יהוה) is transliterated as "YHWH" per LOGOS convention, preserving the divine name as it appears in the Leningrad Codex rather than substituting a title.

2. **"Sons of Israel" (vv. 2, 13, 29):** Hebrew בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל rendered literally as "sons of Israel" rather than the interpretive "Israelites," preserving the patronymic construction.

3. **"Salt Sea" (vv. 3, 12):** Hebrew יָם הַמֶּלַח rendered literally. This is the Dead Sea, but the formal translation preserves the Hebrew designation.

4. **"Ascent of Akrabbim" (v. 4):** Hebrew מַעֲלֵה עַקְרַבִּים. The name means "Scorpion Pass" but is retained in transliterated form as a proper noun, consistent with formal equivalence.

5. **"Brook of Egypt" (v. 5):** Hebrew נַחְלָה מִצְרָיִם. This refers to Wadi el-Arish, the traditional southwestern boundary marker, not the Nile River. The Vaticanus reads χειμάρρουν Αἰγύπτου, confirming "brook/wadi of Egypt."

6. **"Great Sea" (vv. 6, 7):** Hebrew הַיָּם הַגָּדוֹל, the Mediterranean. Vaticanus confirms ἡ θάλασσα ἡ μεγάλη.

7. **"Mount Hor" (vv. 7, 8):** Hebrew הֹר הָהָר. This northern Mount Hor is distinct from the Mount Hor where Aaron died (Num 20:22-29), which was in the south near Edom. The identification is debated; some scholars associate it with a peak in the Lebanon range.

8. **"Entrance of Hamath" (v. 8):** Hebrew לְבוֹא חֲמָת. This phrase marks the ideal northern extent of the promised land (cf. Num 13:21; Josh 13:5; 1 Kgs 8:65). Hamath is modern Hama in Syria.

9. **"Sea of Chinnereth" (v. 11):** Hebrew כִּנֶּרֶת, the Sea of Galilee. The formal translation preserves the ancient Hebrew name. Vaticanus reads Χενερεθ.

10. **"Slope" (v. 11):** Hebrew כֶּתֶף, literally "shoulder," used topographically for the slope or ridge descending toward the lake. Rendered "slope" for clarity while preserving the concrete spatial sense.

11. **"Mark out" (vv. 7, 8, 10):** Hebrew תְּתָאוּ (from תאה), meaning to mark, delineate, or trace a boundary line. This technical surveying term appears in the hitpael, rendered "mark out" to convey the deliberate boundary-setting action.

12. **"Divide the land for an inheritance" (vv. 17, 18):** Hebrew יִנְחֲלוּ, from נחל in the piel, meaning to apportion as an inheritance. The Vaticanus uses κληρονομέω and κατακληρονομέω, confirming the inheritance nuance.

13. **Tribal leader names (vv. 19-28):** All proper names transliterated from the Masoretic vocalization in the Leningrad Codex. The Vaticanus spellings were consulted as secondary witness (e.g., Χαλεβ for Caleb, Σαμουηλ for Shemuel).

14. **Order of tribes (vv. 19-28):** The ten tribes listed correspond to those receiving land west of the Jordan: Judah, Simeon, Benjamin, Dan, Manasseh, Ephraim, Zebulun, Issachar, Asher, Naphtali. Reuben, Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh are excluded, having received territory in Transjordan (vv. 14-15).

15. **"By lot" (v. 13):** Hebrew בְּגוֹרָל, indicating the sacred lot-casting method of land distribution. The Vaticanus confirms κλήρῳ.

16. **Conjunctive "And" (throughout):** The Hebrew waw-consecutive (וַיְדַבֵּר, וְהָיָה, etc.) is consistently rendered with "And" at the start of verses and clauses, preserving the Hebrew narrative connective structure rather than smoothing it into English prose style.
